page: Amazon Web Services m6a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores based on the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Cryptographic capabilities are also highly efficient, as evidenced by a top-tier rating in the PassMark CPU encryption test, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets. Memory performance is strong for cached small-block reads, though large-block read and write operations fall into the average range.

Given its high core density and balanced memory allocation, the m6a.24xlarge is suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, database operations, and medium-scale language model inference. It delivers strong performance in Geekbench compiling and ray tracing tests. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance provides a high-density, dedicated AMD EPYC resource pool without the premium associated with specialized GPU or local NVMe storage hardware, making it a viable option for enterprise-scale general-purpose applications and web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical AMD EPYC 7R13 cores running at 3.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities, alongside strong database and software compilation execution. The hardware configuration is optimized for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, database operations, and medium-scale LLM inference, offering a resource-dense, non-accelerated compute platform.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and AMD EPYC 7R13 processors, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, optimized for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose compute platform featuring ninety-six AMD EPYC vCPUs and balanced memory for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores of AMD EPYC 7R13 processors at 3.6 GHz.
Equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, maintaining a balanced 4:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio.
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and cryptographic performance in benchmark testing.
Features 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU accelerators.
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
